By companion order, the court has sustained defendant Gavin Newsom's demurrer to all causes of action against him and has granted his motion to strike statements in the complaint concerning his alleged ownership of the Balboa Cafe and his political statements and public positions.

The court grants the motion for sanctions, as explained further below, but finds the sanctionable conduct to be only the inclusion of extraneous material about Newsom's public statements and positions that does not relate to the causes of action Doe brought against him

DEFENDANT PLUMPJACK MANAGEMENT GROUP LLC, BALBOA CAFE PARTNERS, HILARY NEWSOM, GAVIN NEWSOM, JEREMY SCHERER, DEANN GRAFFIGNA, GUILLAUME ISSAVERDEN MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT

Defendants move to strike portions of the complaint pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 436, which permits the court to strike from a pleading any "irrelevant, false, or improper matter." (CCP 436(a).) The portions of the complaint defendants seek to strike concern Gavin Newsom and his political statements and public positions. By companion order, the court has sustained Gavin Newsom's demurrer to all causes of action without leave to amend. Accordingly, there is no continuing relevance to the Complaint's statements about him. The court grants the motion to strike the paragraphs and lines specified in Defendants' moving papers. Doe has leave to amend paragraph 69 to assert facts relevant to Hilary Newsom's state of mind. The order prepared by the moving party should incorporate the specific paragraphs and lines from the notice of motion.
